title: protein_variants: A Controlled Experiment in LLM-Assisted Scientific Software Development Using a Blackboard Knowledge Source Architecture for Deterministic Protein Missense Variant Interpretation
description: A deterministic, rule-based structural evidence lookup engine for interpreting protein missense variants, built primarily using large language models (Claude and ChatGPT) as engineering collaborators. The system follows a blackboard architecture with four Knowledge Sources: DomainMapper, StructureMapper, Interpretation, and EvidenceValidator. Variant interpretation is evaluated against MaveDB functional scores and ClinVar clinical classifications. The primary research contribution is the LLM-assisted development workflow and its documented failure modes -- not the variant calls themselves. Forty failure modes are documented across two LLM phases (15 ChatGPT, 25 Claude). The system achieves 100% agreement with peer-reviewed experimental evidence across five canonical TP53 hotspot variants, and produces scientifically correct no_data and ambiguous agreement results for five intermediate/uncertain classification variants. 90 RSpec examples, 0 failures.
notes: Development workflow: Claude (Anthropic) as architect, scientist, and prompt author; Codex CLI as code implementer. Initial phase used ChatGPT (OpenAI); project transferred to Claude after 15 documented failure modes. See CLAUDE_ERRORS.md and CHATGPT_ERRORS.md in the repository.
